title: If the prime lending rate is phased out, what does it mean for consumers?
description: BUSINESS NEWS - For over two decades, the...
bodyText:
[{"type":"contributor","text":"Therese Grobler, Momentum","contributorName":"Therese Grobler, Momentum","contributorDate":"Thursday, 29 January 2026, 14:34"},{"type":"intro_bold","text":"To understand the change, we have to look at the current \"350 basis point\" rule."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"BUSINESS NEWS - For over two decades, the prime lending rate has dictated the cost of consumer finance in South Africa. Whether you’re signing for a new car, a first home, or a personal loan, the conversation almost always revolves around \"prime plus\" or \"prime minus.\"","html":"BUSINESS NEWS - For over two decades, the prime lending rate has dictated the cost of consumer finance in South Africa. Whether you’re signing for a new car, a first home, or a personal loan, the conversation almost always revolves around \"prime plus\" or \"prime minus.\""},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Yet very few consumers are ever encouraged to understand what those terms actually mean, or how interest rates are constructed."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Speaking at Davos, South African Reserve Bank (SARB) Governor Lesetja Kganyago said the prime rate would potentially be phased out in line with the bank’s current reform drive. While this might sound like technical jargon, it presents an important opportunity to improve how consumers understand the true cost of borrowing.","html":"Speaking at Davos, South African Reserve Bank (SARB) Governor Lesetja Kganyago said the prime rate would potentially be phased out in line with the bank’s current reform drive. While this might sound like technical jargon, it presents an important opportunity to improve how consumers understand the true cost of borrowing."},{"type":"heading","text":"What exactly is the prime lending rate?","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"To understand the change, we have to look at the current \"350 basis point\" rule. In South Africa, currently there are two main rates. The repo rate is the interest rate at which the SARB lends money to commercial banks (currently 6.75%). The prime rate is the benchmark rate banks use to lend to the public (currently 10.25%).","html":"To understand the change, we have to look at the current \"350 basis point\" rule. In South Africa, currently there are two main rates. The repo rate is the interest rate at which the SARB lends money to commercial banks (currently 6.75%). The prime rate is the benchmark rate banks use to lend to the public (currently 10.25%)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Since 2001, the gap between these two has been fixed at exactly 3.5% (350 basis points). If the SARB raises the repo rate by 0.25%, prime automatically goes up by 0.25%. While this system has made rate changes easy to follow, it has also meant that many borrowers focus on the outcome (their repayment) rather than understanding how that number is built up.","html":"Since 2001, the gap between these two has been fixed at exactly 3.5% (350 basis points). If the SARB raises the repo rate by 0.25%, prime automatically goes up by 0.25%. While this system has made rate changes easy to follow, it has also meant that many borrowers focus on the outcome (their repayment) rather than understanding how that number is built up."},{"type":"heading","text":"The shift to a repo-plus model","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The SARB governor has indicated that the bank will move toward a repo-plus model. Instead of using a middleman rate like prime, banks would price loans directly against the repo rate. For consumers, this shift is less about higher or lower rates and more about clarity.","html":"The SARB governor has indicated that the bank will move toward a repo-plus model. Instead of using a middleman rate like prime, banks would price loans directly against the repo rate. For consumers, this shift is less about higher or lower rates and more about clarity."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The potential benefits include:","html":"The potential benefits include:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"1. Clearer explanations of interest rates","html":"1. Clearer explanations of interest rates"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Currently, the prime margin combines several factors into a single figure. Under a repo-plus model, banks may explain lending rates more explicitly, showing how the base rate, risk, and service costs contribute to the final rate you pay. This can help consumers better understand why two people may pay different interest rates, even when borrowing similar amounts.","html":"Currently, the prime margin combines several factors into a single figure. Under a repo-plus model, banks may explain lending rates more explicitly, showing how the base rate, risk, and service costs contribute to the final rate you pay. This can help consumers better understand why two people may pay different interest rates, even when borrowing similar amounts."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"2. Easier comparison between offers","html":"2. Easier comparison between offers"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When pricing is expressed directly against the repo rate, it may become simpler for consumers to compare offers across banks. Instead of relying on familiar but abstract terms like “prime plus,” borrowers can focus on how much above the repo rate they are being charged and why.","html":"When pricing is expressed directly against the repo rate, it may become simpler for consumers to compare offers across banks. Instead of relying on familiar but abstract terms like “prime plus,” borrowers can focus on how much above the repo rate they are being charged and why."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"3. Direct monetary policy impact","html":"3. Direct monetary policy impact"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When the SARB changes interest rates, they want the effect to be felt immediately in the economy. Moving to a repo-plus model simplifies this. It removes an unnecessary layer of signalling and gives the SARB a tighter rein on the economy, making it easier for them to accelerate or decelerate consumer spending when necessary.","html":"When the SARB changes interest rates, they want the effect to be felt immediately in the economy. Moving to a repo-plus model simplifies this. It removes an unnecessary layer of signalling and gives the SARB a tighter rein on the economy, making it easier for them to accelerate or decelerate consumer spending when necessary."},{"type":"heading","text":"Will your debt get cheaper?","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A change in the benchmark does not automatically mean a change in the cost of debt. The economics of lending haven't changed. Banks still have to consider individual credit risk, funding costs and operational expenses. What does change is how these elements are communicated to borrowers.","html":"A change in the benchmark does not automatically mean a change in the cost of debt. The economics of lending haven't changed. Banks still have to consider individual credit risk, funding costs and operational expenses. What does change is how these elements are communicated to borrowers."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"If the prime rate is phased out, existing loans are unlikely to change overnight. Monthly repayments will generally remain the same, but borrowers may begin to see interest rates explained in a way that is more transparent and easier to understand."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In turn, this shift encourages consumers to ask the right questions:","html":"In turn, this shift encourages consumers to ask the right questions:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"How is my interest rate calculated?What factors influence the margin I’m being charged?How does my credit behaviour affect the rate I receive?","html":"
  • How is my interest rate calculated?
  • What factors influence the margin I’m being charged?
  • How does my credit behaviour affect the rate I receive?
    • "},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The SARB is expected to manage the transition carefully, ensuring stability while helping consumers adjust to a new way of understanding interest rates.","html":"The SARB is expected to manage the transition carefully, ensuring stability while helping consumers adjust to a new way of understanding interest rates."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In summary, while phasing out Prime is unlikely to lower borrowing costs in the short term, it represents a meaningful step forward for financial literacy.","html":"In summary, while phasing out Prime is unlikely to lower borrowing costs in the short term, it represents a meaningful step forward for financial literacy."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Greater transparency around interest rate pricing empowers consumers to compare products more confidently, understand the true cost of credit, and take a more active role in their financial decision-making.","html":"Greater transparency around interest rate pricing empowers consumers to compare products more confidently, understand the true cost of credit, and take a more active role in their financial decision-making."},{"type":"image_with_caption","text":"Therese Grobler, Head of Wealth Management at Momentum Financial Planning.","imageUrl":"https://cms.groupeditors.com//media/1/1///6da7ca2c-c49e-49ea-a8b8-0b4142e3ee39.jpg","caption":"Therese Grobler, Head of Wealth Management at Momentum Financial Planning."}]

images.alt: If the prime lending rate is phased out, what does it mean for consumers?
If the prime lending rate is phased out, what does it mean for consumers?
images.caption: Therese Grobler, Head of Wealth Management at Momentum Financial Planning.

author: Therese Grobler, Momentum
teaser: To understand the change, we have to look at the current "350 basis point" rule.
featuredCaption: Speaking at Davos, South African Reserve Bank (SARB) Governor Lesetja Kganyago said the prime rate would potentially be phased out in line with the bank’s current reform drive. Image: Canva
